title: Workflow — cross-team SEV-1 with multi-service timeline and escalation
code_snippet:
# User provides: PagerDuty alerts from 3 teams (payments, orders, infrastructure),
# Grafana dashboards showing cascading failures across payment-api, order-service,
# and Redis cluster. Slack threads from #incident-0203, #infra-escalation,
# #payments-oncall. Total outage: 62 minutes. Data: customer-facing 503s,
# order queue backup, Redis master failover. 3 teams responded independently
# with conflicting mitigation attempts.
#
# User says: 'Write a deep post-mortem for yesterday's SEV-1. Multiple teams
# were involved — payments, orders, and infra. 62 min total outage, cascading
# failure from Redis to everything downstream.'
expected_feedback: Workflow: Draft mode + Deep depth (SEV-1, multi-team). (1) Force Deep per §3: SEV-1 + multi-team involvement. (2) Timeline: merge parallel entries from 3 team sources into single UTC timeline, mark which team performed each action. (3) Root cause: 5-Why to systemic level — why did failure cascade across service boundaries? (4) Contributing factors: unclear escalation ownership between teams, conflicting mitigation attempts, no unified incident commander. (5) Action items must span all 3 teams with cross-team owners. (6) SEV-1 requires: action items within 48 hours, exec review, Deep post-mortem.
